For example, if words are ["ab", "cd"], then valid substrings are "abcd" or "cdab". The task is to find all starting positions in s where such a concatenation occurs.

Constraints: s can be up to 10^4 characters, words can be up to 5000 elements. Each word up to 30 characters. So brute-force checking every possible substring is probably not feasible, especially since for each position, checking all permutations would be expensive.

But how to check that efficiently?

The key is to check if the substring can be split into chunks of word length, and each chunk is present in the words list, with the correct count. Also, since words can have duplicates, we need to track frequencies.

So, for example, if words are ["foo", "bar", "foo"], then the concatenated string must contain exactly two "foo" and one "bar".
